metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Prefix the Gerrit-review gate with `[operator-attested]` (e.g. `[operator-attested] Single Gerrit change lands after LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1`) so the rubric exception applies and tooling treats it as an attestation rather than an in-session command.
evidence: The plan states 'single Gerrit change lands only after LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1' as part of the last AC checkbox — no [operator-attested] tag present.
ADR 0043 (referenced in rubric) establishes that criteria whose done-evidence lives outside the codebase must carry the [operator-attested] prefix to avoid being treated as in-session checks.
findings.finding: The final AC bundles 'LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1' as a completion criterion without the [operator-attested] tag, making it appear as an in-session completable check when it actually requires external human reviewer action that an executing agent cannot perform or verify in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria / final bullet: 'make lint + mypy clean; touched test modules pass; single Gerrit change lands only after LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1'
scenarios: An executing agent marks the ticket complete after lint/mypy/tests pass, treating the Gerrit gate as already satisfied — or conversely blocks indefinitely waiting for a human reviewer signal it cannot observe.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the last AC bullet to: `- [operator-attested] Single Gerrit change lands only after LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1 (record change-id and vote timestamp here).`

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the AI-3b bullet which validator/function to call, the input fixture shape (e.g. `category='transform'` plus minimal required fields), and the concrete assertion (e.g. 'no ValidationError raised' or a specific return-value check) so the executor can write the correct test without inference.
evidence: Plan says only: 'add a non-review (category: transform) test proving input-contract validation is category-agnostic' — no function name, no input shape, no assertion.
The AC mirrors this vagueness: 'AI-3b non-review category test added and passing' — observable but silent on what the test must contain.
findings.finding: AI-3b scope does not specify which validator function to call, what inputs to pass, or what assertion to make in the new 'category: transform' test, requiring the executor to infer the full test body from the existing file.
scenarios: An executor writes a trivially passing no-op test (e.g. assert True) that satisfies 'passing' but does not actually exercise any validation path, silently meeting the AC without proving the claim.
An executor calls the wrong validator (wrong depth, wrong entry-point) because the plan doesn't name it.
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the AI-3b bullet to: 'In test_validation_depth.py, add a parametrized or standalone test that calls <ValidatorClass>(<input with category="transform">) and asserts it completes without raising — mirroring the existing review-category test at line N but with category="transform".'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either enumerate '_engine_dir import' explicitly in the Tier-2 symbol list or amend AC4 to read 'Exactly the enumerated Tier-2 symbols and their described orphaned imports removed' so the _engine_dir removal is unambiguously covered.
evidence: Tier-2: 'src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py: _SCRIPTS_DIR (+ its now-orphaned _engine_dir import)'
AC4: 'Exactly the enumerated Tier-2 symbols removed; each verified unreferenced; retained symbols untouched.'
_engine_dir import is described as a side-effect removal in parentheses, not enumerated as its own symbol in the Tier-2 list; AC4's 'exactly enumerated' language may not cover it.
findings.finding: The Tier-2 scope entry for reads.py describes removing both _SCRIPTS_DIR AND 'its now-orphaned _engine_dir import', but the AC4 verifies only 'exactly the enumerated Tier-2 symbols removed' — and _engine_dir import is not enumerated as a named symbol in the Tier-2 list, leaving it ambiguously covered (or not) by AC4.
findings.location: Scope / Tier-2, reads.py entry; Acceptance criteria AC4
scenarios: An implementer reads AC4 strictly, removes only '_SCRIPTS_DIR', and leaves the orphaned _engine_dir import, passing AC4 as written while leaving dead code behind.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the reads.py Tier-2 entry to list two items: '_SCRIPTS_DIR' and '_engine_dir (import, now orphaned after _SCRIPTS_DIR removal)', so AC4's 'exactly enumerated' language covers both.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 fix to `run_differs._read_local_ticket_full` (line 159): apply the same explicit-env construction (build from `os.environ` minus `REBAR_SYNC_PULL` on the live path; set it to `"off"` on the no-sync path) and cover it with a parallel regression assertion.
evidence: reconcile_helpers.py line 387: `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL="off") if no_sync else None`
run_differs.py line 159: `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL="off") if no_sync else None` — identical pattern, identical defect
Plan scope section lists only 'reconcile_helpers.py (AI-1)' — run_differs.py is absent

AC line: 'AI-1 fixed; regression test asserts the live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off' — this AC cannot be fully satisfied if run_differs.py still inherits the ambient env on the live path
findings.finding: run_differs._read_local_ticket_full has the identical AI-1 env-inheritance bug as reconcile_helpers._read_local_tickets but is NOT named as a co-target of the fix, leaving the defect present in a second call site.
scenarios: A caller sets REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off in the environment and then triggers a full reconcile that goes through run_differs._read_local_ticket_full with no_sync=False — the live sync is silently suppressed via the ambient env despite the caller expecting it to run.
The regression test proves the reconcile_helpers path is fixed but run_differs.py silently re-introduces the same failure for per-ticket targeted reads.
findings.suggested_fix: In run_differs._read_local_ticket_full, replace `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL="off") if no_sync else None` with the same explicit-env pattern planned for reconcile_helpers: build a base env from `os.environ`, pop `REBAR_SYNC_PULL` on the live path, set it to `"off"` on the no-sync path. Add a parallel regression test assertion analogous to the one planned for reconcile_helpers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 explicit-env construction to also strip `REBAR_NO_SYNC` on the live path (alongside `REBAR_SYNC_PULL`), and add a second assertion to the regression test verifying that an ambient `REBAR_NO_SYNC=1` is also stripped on the live path.
evidence: Plan: 'strip the key on the live path' — singular, no mention of REBAR_NO_SYNC
AC: 'regression test asserts the live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off' — REBAR_NO_SYNC absent from AC
src/rebar/_config_sources.py:233: '"REBAR_NO_SYNC": ("sync", "pull", "REBAR_SYNC_PULL")' — alias is live in config layer
src/rebar/_deprecations.py:80: '_permanent("env", "REBAR_NO_SYNC", "REBAR_SYNC_PULL")' — permanent alias, never to be removed
findings.finding: The AI-1 env-strip fix addresses REBAR_SYNC_PULL but the plan does not explicitly cover its permanent alias REBAR_NO_SYNC. On the live path, an ambient REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 in the caller's environment would still suppress the sync pull after the fix, because the config layer resolves REBAR_NO_SYNC as a permanent alias for sync.pull=off (_config_sources.py:233, _deprecations.py:80). The regression test AC only mentions REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off, leaving the alias vector untested.
findings.location: Scope / Tier 1 — reconcile_helpers.py (AI-1); AC line: 'live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off'
scenarios: CI runner sets REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 (the older alias still widely used in scripts); the live-path subprocess inherits it and skips the sync pull even after AI-1 is applied, because only REBAR_SYNC_PULL is stripped.
A caller wraps the reconciler with REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 for an outer no-sync operation; inner live-path calls inherit it.
findings.suggested_fix: On the live path build: `_env = {k: v for k, v in _os.environ.items() if k not in ('REBAR_SYNC_PULL', 'REBAR_NO_SYNC')}`. On the no_sync path: `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off')` (stripping REBAR_NO_SYNC is not needed here since REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off takes precedence, but stripping both is also correct). Add a regression test parametrized over both env vars.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan whether `DefaultBatchRunner` and `CodeReviewBatchRunner` should guard against `agent_runner=None` (e.g. fall back to `FakeAgentRunner()`, or assert non-None), or whether the optional signature is base-class-only with subclasses retaining a required parameter (using `@override` + narrowing), to prevent a silent runtime deref.
evidence: Plan: 'make agent_runner optional in the base BatchRunner, DefaultBatchRunner, and CodeReviewBatchRunner too'
src/rebar/llm/workflow/runners.py:153: `out = agent_runner.run(ctx).outputs or {}` — called unconditionally inside DefaultBatchRunner._run_one
src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:73: `out = agent_runner.run(ctx).outputs or {}` — called unconditionally inside CodeReviewBatchRunner.run

By contrast, ProductionBatchRunner immediately `del agent_runner` (production_batch_runner.py:73) making its None-safe path explicit; DefaultBatchRunner and CodeReviewBatchRunner have no analogous guard

The interpreter always passes rc.runner (interpreter.py:388: runner.run(req, rc.runner)) so None is only reachable via direct test/construction calls — but no fallback for that path is specified in the plan
findings.finding: The plan makes `agent_runner` optional (default `None`) in `BatchRunner`, `DefaultBatchRunner`, and `CodeReviewBatchRunner` for mypy consistency, but specifies no fallback or guard for the `agent_runner=None` case in `DefaultBatchRunner._run_one` (which calls `agent_runner.run(ctx)` unconditionally at runners.py:153) and `CodeReviewBatchRunner.run` (which calls `agent_runner.run(ctx)` at batch_runner.py:73). These are agent execution paths that will raise `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'run'` if invoked without an agent runner.
findings.location: Tier 1 / runners.py + code_review/batch_runner.py (#450) / AC: 'BatchRunner.run signatures aligned; mypy clean'
scenarios: A test or external caller constructs DefaultBatchRunner().run(req) or DefaultBatchRunner().run(req, None) — currently a TypeError, would become an AttributeError on NoneType after the signature change, with no specified fallback
CodeReviewBatchRunner constructed and called with agent_runner=None (e.g. in an offline test) will AttributeError when processing any included criterion
findings.suggested_fix: For `DefaultBatchRunner`, change the signature to `agent_runner: AgentStepRunner | None = None` and add `if agent_runner is None: agent_runner = FakeAgentRunner()` at the top of `run()`. For `CodeReviewBatchRunner`, do the same. This mirrors the executor's existing pattern (`runner = FakeAgentRunner() if agent_runner is None else agent_runner` at executor.py:546) and gives the optional path a well-defined fallback.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out statement for the AI-1 reconcile_helpers.py behavioral change (e.g., 'revert the Gerrit change' or a feature-flag approach) so T4's reversibility requirement is met, not just the regression test.
evidence: 'when no_sync=False, _env=None inherits the parent env, so a caller's REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off silently suppresses the live sync pull. Build an explicit env; set REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off only when no_sync=True; strip the key on the live path.'
AC: 'AI-1 fixed; regression test asserts the live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off...'
No rollback/back-out plan mentioned anywhere in the plan text — the Gerrit gate is cited as a quality gate, not a revert mechanism.
findings.finding: The reconcile_helpers.py behavioral change (AI-1) alters runtime environment construction — a side-effecting, environment-dependent path that may behave differently across deployment contexts — yet no explicit back-out or rollback plan is described. The plan has a regression test and a Gerrit gate, but T4 requires an explicit rollback/back-out mechanism, not merely acknowledgement or implicit revertability.
findings.location: Scope / Tier-1, AI-1; Acceptance criteria AC1
scenarios: A deployment environment has REBAR_SYNC_PULL set to a value other than 'off'; after landing, the new explicit-env path strips it, triggering unexpected live-sync behavior not caught by the regression test; the team needs a documented back-out path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line rollback note to the plan: 'Back-out: revert the single Gerrit change; no data migration required. The regression test guards re-introduction.' This satisfies T4 without expand-contract sequencing.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that `_UUID_CREATE`, `_UUID_A`, `_UUID_B`, `_UUID_C` in `tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py` are dead fixture constants safe to remove. In fact, all four are actively used as event-UUID arguments in `_write_event` calls throughout that file (lines 63, 71, 81, 92, 93, 107, 108, 122, 123, 136, 137, 148, 149, 160, 171, 182, 208). Removing them would cause immediate NameError failures across every test in the file.
findings.location: Tier 2 bullet: 'dead test-fixture constants (_UUID_*/_ALIAS_*/_ENGINE_DIR) in … test_reducer_tag_delta'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `test_reducer_tag_delta.py` from the Tier-2 dead-symbol list entirely; its `_UUID_*` constants are live.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that `_ALIAS_TARGET_UUID`, `_ALIAS_LINK_UUID`, and `_ALIAS_SOURCE_UUID` in `tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py` are dead fixture constants safe to remove. In fact, all three are actively used in test bodies: `_ALIAS_TARGET_UUID` appears in `target_ticket_dir = tracker_dir / _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID` (line 499) and in assertions (lines 548, 568–569); `_ALIAS_SOURCE_UUID` and `_ALIAS_LINK_UUID` are defined alongside and imply the same usage context. Removing them would cause NameError.
findings.location: Tier 2 bullet: 'dead test-fixture constants (_UUID_*/_ALIAS_*/_ENGINE_DIR) in … test_reducer_links'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `_ALIAS_*` entries in `test_reducer_links.py` from the Tier-2 removal list; these constants are live.

findings.finding: _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID in test_reducer_links.py is actively used (lines 499, 548, 568-569) and is NOT dead. The plan groups all three _ALIAS_* constants as dead fixtures to remove, but _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID is a live test assertion value; removing it would break the alias-normalization test.
findings.location: Tier 2 scope bullet: 'dead test-fixture constants (_UUID_*/_ALIAS_*/_ENGINE_DIR) in … test_reducer_links'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove only `_ALIAS_LINK_UUID` and `_ALIAS_SOURCE_UUID`; replace their inline uses (if any) with the literal strings and keep `_ALIAS_TARGET_UUID` as-is.

findings.finding: _UUID_A and _UUID_B in test_reducer_tag_delta.py are actively used throughout the test module (lines 63, 71, 81, 92, 93, 107, 108, 122, 123, 136, 137, 148, 149, 160, 171, 182, 208). Only _UUID_C (line 36) is unreferenced. The plan labels all _UUID_* in that file as dead.
findings.location: Tier 2 scope bullet: 'dead test-fixture constants (_UUID_*/_ALIAS_*/_ENGINE_DIR) in … test_reducer_tag_delta'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove only the `_UUID_C` line (line 36) from test_reducer_tag_delta.py.
